name: Black Bean Chili Con Carne with Cilantro Pesto
recipeIngredient: 4 tablespoons ground mild red chilies (ancho or New Mexico)
1 red bell pepper, cored and julienned
6 cups cooked black beans, salt to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut carrots, celery, and bell pepper into strips about 1-1/2 inches long (julienne). Julliene the cheese or grate with long strokes.
2. Brown and crisp the meat: Slice beef into very thin 2-inch strips. Heat 3 tablespoons of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat, and brown beef in batches. Brown the ground pork. When all the meat is well browned, drain off fat and return the meat to the pan. Continue to brown for another few minutes until the meat begins to crisp. Sprinkle with salt, add chili powder, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly. Remove skillet from heat and set aside.
3. Brown vegetables: In a large cast iron pot or casserole, heat remaining 1 tablespoon oil. Sauté onions over medium heat until they begin to wilt, about 5 minutes. Add carrots, celery, and red pepper and sauté for another 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook for an additional minute.
4. Combine: Lower the heat. Scrape meat into pot with onion mixture. Heat skillet again over medium-high heat and pour in wine. Scrape browned bits and reduce wine for a minute or so. Pour wine mixture into casserole with meat and vegetables.
5. Add tomatoes, water, molasses, sage, and cumin to the pot. Simmer, covered, for 1-1/2 hours, stirring occasionally. Add black beans (drained if canned) and season with salt as needed. Cook another 15 minutes.
6. Serve: Spoon cilantro pesto onto each serving and serve garnishes on the side.
